title: CPU-only narrowed setup
description: Record the actual execution environment and explicitly document the narrowed simulator-backed scope.

steps.step1: Capture `uname -a`, Python version, CPU count, RAM, filesystem mounts, and package versions under `artifacts/env/`.
steps.step2: Record missing dependencies and infeasible original-plan items: `filebench`, `fio`, `hyperfine`, live kernel page-cache policy swapping, and a SQLite VFS page logger.
steps.step3: Use exactly three seeds `[11, 17, 23]` and a CPU-only runtime plan with 2 cores and an 8-hour wall-clock budget.
steps.step4: Create the required workspace layout under `artifacts/`, `exp/`, and `figures/`.

title: Measured workload execution and trace capture
description: Generate three workload families from real execution while narrowing the data-collection claim to what is feasible here.

steps.step1: Use exactly three workload families: `stream_scan`, `sqlite_zipf`, and `filebench_fileserver`.
steps.step2: For `stream_scan`, build a measured file-corpus scan with periodic hot rereads and log the logical page stream directly from the generator.
steps.step3: For `sqlite_zipf`, run a measured read-mostly SQLite workload with point updates under skew, logging logical page IDs derived from fixed-width row packing rather than a VFS shim.
steps.step4: For `filebench_fileserver`, run a measured Python fileserver-like workload with reads, writes, and metadata events because `filebench` is unavailable.
steps.step5: Capture `ExtendedHinted` as `{logical_seq, inode_id, page_index, op_class, phase_id, cache_insert_seen, cache_evict_seen, dirty_or_writeback_seen, reclaim_epoch}`.
steps.step6: Populate `cache_insert_seen` and `cache_evict_seen` from sparse kernel-visible `mincore` residency transitions where feasible, not from a replay-side observer; populate `reclaim_epoch` from coarse `/proc/vmstat` sampling.
steps.step7: Derive `CompactState`, `NoDirty`, `AccessOnly`, and supplementary `NoReclaim` traces from the same executions and save workload statistics to `artifacts/tables/workload_stats.csv`.

title: Fixed six-policy comparison pool
description: Keep the candidate pool fixed and report simple, strong, and deployed baselines fairly.

steps.step1: Replay exactly six policies on every workload, seed, and budget: `LinuxDefault`, `FIFO`, `CLOCK`, `LFU`, `S3FIFO`, and `Hyperbolic`.
steps.step2: Treat `FIFO` as the simple baseline, `S3FIFO` as the strong baseline, and `LinuxDefault` as the deployed-systems baseline.
steps.step3: Use deterministic implementations and fixed policy hyperparameters across workloads.
steps.step4: Use two replay budgets for every workload-seed pair: `0.40 x working_set_estimate` and `0.60 x working_set_estimate`.

title: State-ablation ranking study
description: Measure ranking degradation as observable state is removed.

steps.step1: Run the main `3 workloads x 3 seeds x 2 budgets x 6 policies x 4 trace modes` replay matrix.
steps.step2: Treat `ExtendedHinted` as the primary replay reference and compute ranking by `miss_rate`, then `writeback_count`, then `mean_eviction_age`.
steps.step3: Compute `Kendall_tau_6`, `Spearman_rho_6`, `top1_agreement`, `top2_set_recall`, and `best_policy_regret` for each ablated mode versus `ExtendedHinted`.
steps.step4: Aggregate per-case, per-workload-family, and global results into `artifacts/tables/ranking_metrics.csv`.

title: Reference adequacy, online anchors, and component ablations
description: Validate the richer replay reference conservatively and complete the missing ablation work.

steps.step1: Run `ReferenceStability` by rebuilding the rich replay without explicit insert or evict hints on the full 18-case grid.
steps.step2: Run the supplementary `NoReclaim` ablation on the full 18-case grid and report deltas relative to `CompactState`.
steps.step3: Run one measured online anchor per workload family at seed `11` and budget ratio `0.40`, comparing online ranking against `ExtendedHinted`, `CompactState`, and `AccessOnly`.
steps.step4: Save reference adequacy metrics, ablation deltas, and online-anchor metrics to `artifacts/tables/`.

title: Paired uncertainty and audits
description: Use paired resampling for ablation deltas and run explicit consistency and reference audits.

steps.step1: Compute bootstrap `95 percent` intervals with `1000` paired resamples over the 18 replay cases for compact-versus-ablation comparisons.
steps.step2: Compute bootstrap intervals over the 3 online anchors for live-agreement metrics.
steps.step3: Run a reference audit against official venue or publisher pages where reachable and save the results to `artifacts/tables/reference_audit.csv`.
steps.step4: Run a consistency audit across `proposal.md`, `idea.json`, and `plan.json`, with the proposal and plan explicitly reflecting the narrowed simulator-backed scope.

title: Figures, tables, and aggregated results
description: Regenerate paper artifacts from executed runs only.

steps.step2: Write the required CSV tables under `artifacts/tables/`, including workload statistics, ranking metrics, live-anchor metrics, pruning results, ablations, runtime accounting, and reference audit.
steps.step3: Write a root-level `results.json` whose numbers are derived only from executed experiment outputs.
